channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Around 14 percent of Minnesotans have filed their 2024 taxes so far
item.description: Two months out from the tax filing deadline, the Minnesota Department of Revenue is already beginning to see a stream of return filings. The state is encouraging people to file electronically and pick the direct deposit option if they’re looking to get their returns quickly.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Top Trump official teases 2026 bid for California governor if Harris jumps in race
item.description: Richard Grenell, a longtime Donald Trump loyalist who is serving as U.S. envoy for special missions in the president's second administration, is floating a potential bid for California governor if former Vice President Kamala Harris also runs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury watchdog begins audit of Musk DOGE team's access to federal government's payment system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Treasury Department's Office of Inspector General on Friday said it was launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government's payment system, after Democratic senators raised red flags about the access provided to Trump aide Elon Musk's Department of Governme
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guarded optimism in India as Trump and Modi outline plans to deepen defense partnership
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — There was guarded optimism among military experts in India as Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump outlined plans to ramp up sales of defense systems to New Delhi, including F-35 stealth fighter jets, to deepen the U.S.-India strategic relationship.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary's Orbán predicts Trump will bring Russia back into Western fold after end of Ukraine war
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump's administration will reconnect Russia with the economies and energy networks of Western countries if the war in Ukraine, now nearly three years old, comes to an end, Hungary's nationalist prime minister said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top moments from Linda McMahon's confirmation hearing
item.description: Linda McMahon's Senate confirmation hearing Thursday featured protests, DEI debates, discussions on trans athletes and scrutiny over DOGE spending cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Dem lawmakers answer USAID funding questions after bombshell report
item.description: Fox News Digital asked Democrat lawmakers their thoughts about controversial USAID funding, such as money ending up in the hands of terrorist-aligned groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Trump Setting Records
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA)—California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Modi meet at Oval Office as US weighs tariffs
item.description: Before speaking with Trump, Modi met separately with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesotans, other politicians react to Sen. Tina Smith's decision not to run again
item.description: Minnesota politicians from both sides of the aisle have weighed in on the news that U.S. Sen. Tina Smith, who has served in the Senate since 2018, is retiring after next year.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Watch live: House panel convenes hearing on USAID amid DOGE overhaul
item.description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee is holding a hearing Thursday morning looking into what GOP leadership has called the "betrayal" of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID).
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmaker wants to ban new billboard signs
item.description: Sen. John Marty, DFL-Roseville, said the signs detract from the state's natural scenic beauty and pose a safety concern for motorists. Billboard companies say the bill would hurt local businesses and curtail First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Mobile pharmacy brings meds to Winthrop customers after losing brick and mortar store
item.description: Some Minnesota pharmacies are closing their doors because of lower reimbursement rates for prescription drugs and dwindling profit margins — especially for pharmacies with a large customer base on public insurance.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Skyrocketing fees: St. Pete condo owners say they never asked for bailout, only relief
item.description: Condo owners are waiting for answers on whether lawmakers will do anything to ease concerns over rising fees.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on RFK Jr. to lead HHS
item.description: The 53-47 vote came as Democrats have been forcing Republicans to use up all debate time on President Trump’s most controversial nominees.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard awaits Senate vote on director of national intelligence nomination
item.description: Her chances of confirmation increased when Republicans fell in line following a pressure campaign by Trump allies.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer: Democrats will use spending bills to curb Musk
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) says Democrats will push for language in the spending bills that need to pass by March 14 that would unwind Elon Musk’s efforts to dismantle government agencies such as the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin says unidentified Russian freed in U.S. in exchange for Moscow's release of Marc Fogel
item.description: A Kremlin spokesman told reporters that the unidentified individual would return to Russia “in the coming days,” and his name would be revealed when he returns home — unlike during past prisoner exchanges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump official targeting Jan. 6 investigators worked on those cases himself
item.description: The disconnect between Bove's aggressive stance at the time to hold rioters accountable — and his current hostility towards the Jan. 6 probe — has troubled some former colleagues.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Egg prices unite Nevada lawmakers as AB171 makes its way toward approval
item.description: Nevada lawmakers continued Tuesday to fast-track legislation that could bring down the price of eggs, pushing Assembly Bill 171 (AB171) closer to law.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gov. Hochul reacts to pending dismissal of corruption case against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The pending dismissal of the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has partners in running the city and political opponents raising concerns about whether Adams can run the city. Gov. Kathy Hochul even said she does not know if the mayor is “compromised.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Florida lawmakers to begin third special session on illegal immigration
item.description: After a particularly contentious few weeks, Florida lawmakers will be heading back to Tallahassee Tuesday for the purpose of passing legislation on illegal immigration — this time with the governor's support.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: HIV infections could jump over 6 times if US support dropped & not replaced, UNAIDS chief says
item.description: The head of the U.N. AIDS agency said Monday the number of new HIV infections could jump more than six times by 2029 if American support of the biggest AIDS program is dropped, warning that millions of people could die and more resistant strains of the disease could emerge.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
